description: Show Secondary Meanings in Reverse Practice Session - Display secondary accepted meanings below the primary meaning on reverse practice question and incorrect feedback screens

userStories.title: Add helper function to extract secondary accepted meanings
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a helper function to extract secondary accepted meanings from a Meaning[] array so that both the question and incorrect feedback screens can reuse it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `getSecondaryMeanings` function in ReversePracticeScreen.tsx alongside the existing `getPrimaryMeaning` function (around line 216-219)
The function filters meanings where `accepted_answer: true` and `primary: false`
Returns a comma-separated string of secondary meaning texts (e.g., 'to consume, to have')
Returns an empty string when there are no secondary meanings
Function is covered by unit tests
All tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Show secondary meanings on reverse practice question screen
userStories.description: As a learner, I want to see secondary meanings below the primary meaning during reverse practice so that I have full context about which word is being asked.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary meaning continues to display in large, bold text as it does today
Secondary accepted meanings display below the primary meaning in smaller text (e.g., FONT_SIZES.md or FONT_SIZES.sm)
Secondary meanings text has reduced opacity (e.g., 0.7) or lighter weight to create clear visual hierarchy
Secondary meanings are comma-separated on a single line
When there are no secondary meanings, nothing extra is shown (no visual change for single-meaning items)
Text does not overflow or clip within the meaningContainer for items with many meanings
Add new styles to the existing StyleSheet.create() block
Changes are covered by unit/integration tests
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Show secondary meanings on incorrect feedback screen
userStories.description: As a learner, when I answer incorrectly during reverse practice, I want to see the secondary meanings alongside the primary meaning so I understand the full scope of the word I got wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: Incorrect feedback header shows primary meaning in the existing style
Secondary accepted meanings display below the primary meaning in smaller text, matching the style from US-002
When there are no secondary meanings, nothing extra is shown
Changes are covered by unit/integration tests
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 and US-002 (reuses same helper and styles)
